item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It wasn't aggressive. When attacked, it just tried to run away.

The narration describes, in detached dispassionate language, its progressive injuries as it is shot repeatedly, helpless, dying.

It will barely be remembered, and never be mourned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I knew I was gonna give this one a +1 the second I saw the title was a Freeman's Mind reference

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how the crab doesn't even get a backstory. It simply exists, and by virtue of that it generates conflict. It's my favorite kind of SCP narrative in a nutshell. +1

item.title: time for crab
item.content:encoded: Reminds me a bit of the "Red Death" from Fallout 4, albeit a bit more worthy of the name. +1

item.title: Re: Novote.
item.content:encoded: Alright, I fixed most of what you suggested.

item.title: Downvote.
item.content:encoded:
+ My opinions are inside here
- HIDE

under a Class-33 cover story "Endangered Species Nesting Ground".

Under the Class-33 cover story. Reads quite a lot better, at least to me.

resembling a Cancer productus

My preferred way to writing animal names is the layman's term, followed by the scientific term in brackets. For this, it would be 'resembling a red rock crab (Cancer productus)'.

It possesses an extremely slow metabolism and hunger system

What's a hunger system? Do you mean digestive system? Or do you mean that it only becomes hungry very occasionally? If it's the former, use that term instead. If the latter, this part can probably be cut, as it's shown in the next clause of the sentence.

Addendum 4327.1 Discovery

This is admittedly more of a personal opinion, but I dislike the use of italics here, and also the use of the '.' in the addendum. Generally, I would format things like this as Addendum XXXX-1: Discovery.

Although sound is present in the footage, speech is unintelligible due to poor sound quality.

I don't like the repetition of 'sound' here. Maybe replace the first usage of it with 'audio'?

Minor issues aside, I'm just not the biggest fan of the concept. It's a big crab, which is cool. But the narrative doesn't really do much for me. Militaries get scared of the big crab, so they kill it. There's not really much nuance.

I appreciated the historical context, but it still didn't do quite enough for me. I'm sticking with a novote downvote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Poor Pau Amma - he'll never play with the Sea again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Are you talking about the header bar on the top?

Look here then: http://www.scp-wiki.net/anomaly-classification-system-guide

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i've seen this sort of format multiple times and I can't ascertain as to how the headers and colors are made. If I were to recreate this format, how would I go about it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Attack the weak point for massive damage!

Anyway thank you for the people on the IRC chat for helping me out.

Thank you to rattles for the forum crit.
